action.reasoning: Observation: training now reaches the loss computation and fails because MultiResolutionSTFTLoss expects shape [B,C,T], while the model and targets are [B,T]. Analysis: this is a straightforward tensor-shape bug; the rest of the pipeline is likely fine. To satisfy the required early eval quickly, I should minimally fix train.py by unsqueezing channel dim for MRSTFT and SI-SDR consistently, save a checkpoint after a short run, smoke-test predict.py locally, and submit eval immediately. Decision: patch train.py loss/eval shapes and checkpoint saving, run 1 quick epoch on a subset, test predict.py on a few validation files, then submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 1 --train_limit 512 --val_limit 128 --batch_size 8 --num_workers 2 && mkdir -p tmp_test/noisy && cp /data/val_noisy/*.wav tmp_test/noisy/ && ls tmp_test/noisy | head -n 3 > tmp_test/test_files.txt && python predict.py --data_path tmp_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output tmp_pred.json
